commands.review_summary:
The branch changes queued embedded-agent compaction so owning context engines compact before a bounded Codex/native harness follow-up, clears stale Codex projection metadata before that follow-up, and adds focused regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +139, Tests +136. Total +275 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main can return the Codex non-manual native-compaction skip before an owning context engine compacts. The PR body also includes current-main regression output, but I did not rerun tests because this review is read-only.

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Git protocol-control variables to the shared host environment blocklist, updates generated macOS policy parity, and adds sanitizer/Git regression coverage with a value-aware inherited `GIT_PROTOCOL_FROM_USER` exception.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests +89, Generated +4, Other +18. Total +136 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: PR head adds `GIT_ALLOW_PROTOCOL` to a value-blind inherited blocklist, and Git docs plus focused probes show a restrictive allowlist blocks `git://` while unsetting it lets Git attempt that transport.
commands.review_followup: - The PR has a protected maintainer label and a security-boundary policy choice; maintainers should decide the canonical Git protocol-control behavior after the restrictive allowlist defect is addressed.
